name: Rosemary Leg of Lamb with Roasted Compote
recipeIngredient: 7 pounds bone-in leg of lamb, trimmed, at room temperature
2 cans (14- 1/2oz) diced tomatoes in juice, undrained
1 medium red onion, quartered, thinly sliced
12 brine-cured olives (black, green or combination), pitted and chopped (1/4 cup)
recipeInstructions:
LAMB AND SEASONING: Place racks in middle and bottom positions in oven; preheat oven to 450°F. Place lamb on rack in a large roasting pan. Combine oil, rosemary, garlic, salt, and pepper; rub paste over lamb. Roast on middle oven rack for 15 minutes.
COMPOTE: Meanwhile, in a medium roasting pan, combine tomatoes, onion, 2 tablespoons olive oil, garlic, and pepper. Place on bottom oven rack. Reduce temperature to 350°F. Roast lamb and tomato mixture, rotating the pan once, until internal temperature of thickest part of lamb registers 145°F on an instant-read thermometer for medium-rare (the shank end will be medium). Transfer lamb to a cutting board; tent loosely with foil; let stand 15 minutes; carve. Stir remaining oil, olives, sugar, vinegar, and capers into the tomato mixture. Serve with the lamb.
Makes 8 servings.
